human_verification.notes: Verified issue #1011 scope, NAICS 561 industry specificity, service-support-and-resolution workflow coverage, source-class coverage, scenario-to-ability mapping, refusal triggers, tenant retrieval dependencies, and specification-based runtime boundary.

review_notes: All 12 scenarios pass with explicit shared-service catalog, queue, SLA, runbook, entitlement, evidence, communication, handoff, and refusal behavior.
Required abilities across shared, service-support-and-resolution, and cross-industry profiles are fully covered and mapped.

Scenarios include normal in-scope execution, missing request facts, unsupported exception, repeat failure, adjacent-lane handoff, industry overgeneralization refusal, evidence gaps, low-confidence handling, source conflict, tenant adaptation, and terminology normalization.

specialist_owned_evidence: NAICS 561 administrative support and office administrative services materially shape shared-service scope, artifacts, and refusal behavior.
Shared-service requests are resolved only with service catalog, SOW, entitlement, queue, SLA, runbook, approval, source-record, and audit evidence.
Unsupported workarounds, policy exceptions, repeat failures, source conflicts, and missing tenant facts are escalated rather than resolved informally.
Shared-service coordination does not become authority over legal, finance, HR, compliance, privacy, procurement, technical, safety, records governance, or client management decisions.
Runtime remains specification-based, with no retained-implementation claim embedded in this package.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains after converting tenant variation to explicit retrieval, refusal, and handoff requirements.
Pack is ready for validated specification-based deployment with clear promotion criteria to retained-implementation execution.
